title: Augmented Human Development Index vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living. GDP per capita is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index vs. GDP per capita

The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living. GDP per capita is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot displays the relationship between the Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) and GDP per capita for the year 2020. The vertical axis measures the AHDI on a linear scale from 0 to 0.8, while the horizontal axis represents GDP per capita in international dollars at 2011 prices, plotted on a logarithmic axis ranging from $1,000 to $100,000. Each circle represents a country, with size corresponding to population. Points are color-coded by region: North America (orange), South America (maroon), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (light blue). A positive correlation is visible across the dataset. Countries in the upper-right quadrant, such as Switzerland, the United States, Japan, and France, exhibit high AHDI scores above 0.7 and GDP per capita values exceeding $30,000. In contrast, nations in the lower-left quadrant, including Burundi, Chad, the Democratic Republic of Congo, and Liberia, show AHDI scores below 0.3 and GDP per capita under $2,000. A cluster of countries including India, Pakistan, and Cambodia sits in the lower-middle range with AHDI values around 0.3 to 0.4 and GDP per capita between $5,000 and $10,000. Large circles indicate populous nations like China and India, which occupy the middle of the distribution. Qatar appears as an outlier on the far right with a GDP per capita near $100,000 but an AHDI score around 0.4. European nations generally cluster in the upper section of the chart, while African nations are predominantly located in the lower section.
